prompt:
You are an expert LLVM compiler engineer, specializing in LLVM IR, InstCombine, and missed-optimization analysis.

I found a **new missed optimization in InstCombine** and currently have **only one LLVM IR example**.

Your tasks:

1) Understand the missed optimization from my single example.
2) Generalize it into a reusable pattern and summarize the pattern so it could be implemented as an InstCombine fold.
3) Generate many additional LLVM IR test cases that are semantically equivalent and should trigger the same transformation, including corner cases.

## Reference: common missed elements and patterns

When generalizing, consider the following summary of elements frequently missed in InstCombine and patterns that are often not well handled. 

The original issue is a boolean-`select` canonicalization opportunity:  
`%a = select %x, %y, false` computes `%x && %y`, and `%r = select %a, true, %y` computes `(%x && %y) || %y`, which simplifies to just `%y` by absorption. So the motivating bug was that LLVM recognized the single nested-select form poorly, even though it is just `or(and(X,Y),Y)`.

The patch generalizes this in `simplifySelectInst` for safe boolean `select` forms when `TrueVal == 1` (logical OR shape: `select Cond, true, FalseVal`):  
- If `Cond` matches `logical_and(FalseVal, anything)` in either operand order, return `FalseVal`. This captures `(X && Y) || Y -> Y`.  
- If `FalseVal` matches `logical_and(Cond, anything)` in either operand order, return `Cond`. This captures `Y || (X && Y) -> Y`.  
Use of `m_c_LogicalAnd` is the key abstraction: it handles commuted operands and works over canonical boolean/logical forms rather than just one exact select nest.

`dev_tests` broaden coverage well beyond the motivating case:  
- both nesting/operand orders (`land_lor_left1/2`, `or_and_common_op_commute*`),  
- scalar and `<N x i1>` vector forms,  
- direct logical ops (`and`) and compare-produced booleans (`or_cmps`),  
- poison-sensitive lanes/constants (`*_poison`, `inseltpoison`),  
- degenerate identities like `select %x, false, %x -> false`,  
- and non-matching near-misses (`or_and_not_common_op`) to show the transform requires a shared operand.  

This is a good template: lift a single select-of-select case into a logical matcher, then test all commutations, type widths, and poison cases.

## Output format requirement (strict)

Return **ONLY** JSON (a single JSON object). No Markdown, no extra text.

## What I will provide

- The original LLVM IR snippet (minimal reproducer).
- The target triple/datalayout if relevant. 

## What you must output (in JSON)

Include the following top-level keys (you may add more keys if useful):

- "pattern" (generalized pattern description)
- "testcases" (an array of testcase objects; include only positive cases)

Each testcase object must include at least:

- "name"
- "ir" (the input IR for this case)
- "expected_optimized_ir" (what InstCombine should produce, at least the key simplified instructions; full function preferred)

---

## Here is my single example 

define i1 @test(i1 %x, i1 %y) {
  %a = select i1 %x, i1 %y, i1 false
  %r = select i1 %a, i1 true, i1 %y
  ret i1 %r
}

define i1 @test(i1 %x, i1 %y) {
  ret i1 %y
}

pattern.name: bool_select_absorption_logical_or_with_and
pattern.summary: Fold boolean select patterns that form an OR with an AND sharing a common operand, using absorption: (A && B) || B -> B and B || (A && B) -> B.

instcombine_form.root_shape: select i1/vector<... x i1> Cond, true, FalseVal

folds.match: Cond matches logical_and(FalseVal, X) or logical_and(X, FalseVal)
FalseVal matches logical_and(Cond, X) or logical_and(X, Cond)

matcher_notes: Use a logical boolean matcher such as m_c_LogicalAnd rather than matching one exact nested select shape.
Applies to scalar i1 and vector-of-i1 forms lane-wise.
The logical_and operand may itself be represented as 'and i1', 'select i1 C, i1 V, i1 false', compare-produced i1, or equivalent canonical boolean forms accepted by logical matchers.
Commuted operand orders of the AND must be handled.
Commuted placement of the shared operand between Cond and FalseVal must be handled.
safety_notes: Restrict to boolean semantics: i1 or vector of i1.
Preserve poison semantics by relying on logical boolean matching already considered safe by InstCombine.
Do not apply to near-misses without a shared operand.

testcases.name: nested_select_and_or_basic
nested_select_and_or_commuted_inner
direct_and_in_cond
direct_and_in_cond_commuted
and_on_false_arm
and_on_false_arm_commuted
cmp_and_select
testcases.ir:
define i1 @cmp_and_select(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
  %p = icmp eq i32 %a, %b
  %q = icmp slt i32 %c, %d
  %t = and i1 %p, %q
  %r = select i1 %t, i1 true, i1 %q
  ret i1 %r
}

testcases.name: cmp_nested_select
testcases.ir:
define i1 @cmp_nested_select(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
  %p = icmp eq i32 %a, %b
  %q = icmp slt i32 %c, %d
  %t = select i1 %p, i1 %q, i1 false
  %r = select i1 %t, i1 true, i1 %q
  ret i1 %r
}

testcases.name: triple_operand_common_rhs
triple_operand_common_lhs
vector_and_in_cond_v2
vector_and_in_cond_v4_commuted
vector_nested_select_v2
vector_and_on_false_arm_v4
poison_true_arm_scalar
poison_false_arm_scalar
constant_true_common_operand
constant_false_common_operand
shared_operand_is_select_result
shared_operand_is_cmp_result
testcases.ir:
define i1 @shared_operand_is_cmp_result(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
  %y = icmp ult i32 %a, %b
  %x = icmp ne i32 %c, 0
  %t = and i1 %y, %x
  %r = select i1 %t, i1 true, i1 %y
  ret i1 %r
}

  ret i1 %r
}

#0:
